top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic OCBC Cycle Singapore's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
OCBC Cycle Singapore's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 25 miles.
25 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
OCBC Cycle Singapore's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At OCBC Cycle Singapore, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
At OCBC Cycle Singapore, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of OCBC Cycle Singapore. Train it until it feels boring.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what OCBC Cycle Singapore's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, VO2max, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 25 miles of mixed terrain in Singapore Sports Hub, Singapore.
